page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for compute-heavy workloads requiring parallel processing capabilities. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 368.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.67 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with four NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs, delivering a combined 128 GB of VRAM.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%, while single-threaded gzip compression is poor, in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both reach average levels. Memory bandwidth also performs in the average tier, with read-only large block speeds at 98.85 GB/sec and write speeds at 47.39 GB/sec. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput similarly align with average mid-tier performance.

The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU resources and dedicated CPU cores positions this instance for specialized workloads where parallel GPU compute justifies the hardware class, despite the lower single-core CPU efficiency. It is suitable for deep learning training, GPU-accelerated data science, parallel scientific simulations, and multi-threaded rendering tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 CPU cores on the x86_64 architecture, 368.0 GB of system memory, and four NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs providing 128 GB of total VRAM. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps, though it lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between weak single-core CPU capabilities and average multi-core and multi-threaded performance, with memory bandwidth and Redis operations also scoring in the average tier. Given its high GPU density and dedicated compute resources, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that heavily leverage parallel GPU processing rather than single-threaded CPU speed. It is best suited for deep learning, parallel simulations, and GPU-accelerated data processing.

48 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 CPU cores on x86_64 architecture
